title: NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard
industry: All sectors in NIS2 Annex I and II
exclusions: Does not auto-remediate controls, approve policy exceptions, or certify formal external audits; it only reflects curator-maintained CSVs and Splunk-stored evidence exports available to the search tier at run time.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.57 NIS2 Maturity Benchmark`, Dashboard Studio board `nis2-maturity-benchmark`, and nightly CSV snapshot archived to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search` with RFC 3161 sealing.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.20(1) receives contributing assurance because UC evidence proves the monitorable workflow or telemetry portion while legal approval and filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.20(1) using NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search for NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard, dashboard panel, and archived result export in index=audit_evidence for Art.20(1).

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(f) receives contributing assurance because UC evidence proves the monitorable workflow or telemetry portion while legal approval and filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.21(2)(f) using NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search for NIS2 crawl/walk/run maturity and next-best-control benchmark dashboard, dashboard panel, and archived result export in index=audit_evidence for Art.21(2)(f).

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture `sample-data/uc-22.2.57-fixture.json` sets `uc_id=22.2.57-DEMO` with `status=crawl`, `assurance=high`, `last_reviewed=2024-01-15`, and omits any `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export` rows in the last 120 days so `evidence_age_days=999` and `next_best_control` recommends refreshing exports while `benchmark_rank` sorts near the top.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same obligation row but `status=run`, `last_reviewed` within 30 days, and a fresh `evidence:export` event at `_time=now()-1d` carrying `evidence_id=EVID-778899` yields `weighted_maturity≥90`, causing the sort command to leave the row outside the leadership alert threshold window (top 10) used by the alerting macro.

dataSources: `| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv` carries `clause`, `coverage`, `assurance`, and `uc_id` rows maintained by the compliance office; `| inputlookup uc_implementation_status.csv` layers implementation telemetry `status` (crawl/walk/run), `wave`, `last_reviewed`, and `owner` for each `uc_id`; fresh attestations land in `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export` with `evidence_id`, `exported_at`, and `freshness_score` extracted at index-time. Optional normalisation uses Splunk Common Information Model Add-on ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) when multiple teams push differently named timestamp fields into the same index.
app: Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ search heads orchestrate `savedsearches.conf` schedules over CSV knowledge objects; Splunk Common Information Model Add-on ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)) is optional glue for consistent field aliases (`exported_at` → `_time`) when evidence exports originate from more than one TA.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv
| join type=left uc_id [
    | inputlookup uc_implementation_status.csv
]
| join type=left uc_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(_time) as evidence_export_ts latest(evidence_id) as latest_evidence_id max(freshness_score) as freshness_score by uc_id
]
| eval evidence_age_days=if(isnotnull(evidence_export_ts), round((now()-evidence_export_ts)/86400,2), 999)
| eval review_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_reviewed) AND match(last_reviewed, "^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}"), strptime(last_reviewed, "%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval review_lag_days=if(isnotnull(review_epoch), round((now()-review_epoch)/86400,2), 999)
| eval maturity_score=case(
    status="run" AND evidence_age_days<=30 AND review_lag_days<=90, 95,
    status="walk" AND evidence_age_days<=45 AND review_lag_days<=120, 75,
    status="crawl" AND evidence_age_days<=60, 55,
    true(), 35)
| eval assurance_weight=case(lower(assurance)="high", 1.15, lower(assurance)="medium", 1.0, true(), 0.85)
| eval weighted_maturity=round(maturity_score*assurance_weight,0)
| eval next_best_control=case(
    evidence_age_days>60 AND lower(assurance)="high", "Schedule fresh evidence export + owner attestation for ".uc_id,
    weighted_maturity<50, "Advance ".uc_id." implementation wave from ".wave." with measurable testing",
    review_lag_days>180, "Force quarterly leadership review for ".clause,
    true(), "Maintain current maturity trajectory for ".owner)
| eval benchmark_rank=weighted_maturity*(100/min(100,evidence_age_days))
| sort - benchmark_rank
| table clause, coverage, assurance, uc_id, status, wave, owner, evidence_age_days, review_lag_days, maturity_score, weighted_maturity, next_best_control, latest_evidence_id, freshness_score, benchmark_rank
description: Materialises the obligation catalogue from `nis2_obligation_matrix.csv`, left-joins `uc_implementation_status.csv` for crawl/walk/run status and accountable `owner`, enriches each `uc_id` with the newest `evidence:export` payload from `index=audit_evidence`, derives `evidence_age_days` and `review_lag_days`, multiplies baseline maturity by an `assurance` weight, and ranks `next_best_control` remediation text so leadership sees which duties are slipping before external assurance interviews.
value: Executives trade static compliance spreadsheets for a single prioritized queue that aligns scarce remediation capacity with the riskiest assurance gaps—preventing duplicate projects, shortening audit prep cycles, and showing year-over-year improvement narratives to stakeholders without manually re-scoring hundreds of controls each quarter.
implementation: (1) Publish and version both CSV lookups via change control. (2) Land automated evidence exports on `index=audit_evidence`. (3) Schedule this SPL daily and push results to a Dashboard Studio JSON board for CISO staff review.
visualization: Heat strip for `weighted_maturity`, bubble chart (`benchmark_rank` vs `assurance`), sortable table with drilldown to `latest_evidence_id`, owner filter tokens sourced from `uc_implementation_status.csv`.
dataModelAcceleration: No CIM DMA required; if evidence exports are later routed through accelerated data models, add a tstats variant as `cimSpl` in a future revision.

references.title: Splunk Common Information Model Add-on on Splunkbase

knownFalsePositives: 1) **CSV publication lag after a major control uplift weekend** — engineers finished remediations but the `uc_implementation_status.csv` export still shows `crawl`. Distinguish: Git commit timestamp on the lookup bundle in the deployment repo is newer than Splunk `_reload` time. Suppress: time-bound exception row in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `uc_id` until GRC republishes. 2) **Evidence exports paused during vault maintenance** — `evidence_age_days` spikes although manual PDFs were archived offline. Distinguish: ticketing flag `evidence_pipeline=paused` on `CHG` records. Suppress: join `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change` and filter `where change_freeze=0`. 3) **Duplicate `uc_id` rows after catalogue reorganisation** two benchmarks appear for the same control family. Distinguish: `| inputlookup nis2_obligation_matrix.csv | stats dc(uc_id) by clause` >1. Suppress: deduplicate with `nis2_uc_duplicate_map.csv` lookup keeping canonical `uc_id`. 4) **Owner field lists a distribution list alias** distorting accountability narratives. Distinguish: `owner` contains `DL-` prefix or `@` mailing list domains. Suppress: lookup `nis2_owner_alias.csv` mapping lists to named individuals for reporting only. 5) **Assurance label typo (`High` vs `high`)** reduces weighting unpredictably. Distinguish: rogue casing in CSV. Suppress: normalize with `| eval assurance=lower(assurance)` in a macro or enforce validation during CSV ingest via Git pre-commit hook.
equipmentModels: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (REST API for saved search inventory, _internal for scheduler health)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, correlation search and content management audit)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This UC implements a maturity measurement overlay across the entire NIS2 use case catalogue (22.2.x series). It does not directly satisfy a specific NIS2 article — instead, it measures the entity's progress through the crawl/walk/run implementation waves and identifies the next-best-control to implement for maximum compliance improvement. It is a meta-UC that helps the CISO prioritise NIS2 investment.
• **Maturity model**: the NIS2 use cases are tagged with a `wave` field (crawl, walk, run):
  - **Crawl**: foundational controls that every entity must implement first. These cover the most critical Art.21 requirements with the simplest data sources (lookups, _internal, basic authentication logs). Target: complete within 3 months of NIS2 programme start.
  - **Walk**: intermediate controls that require additional data sources (vulnerability scanners, SBOM, PAM, OT sensors) and more complex SPL. Target: complete within 6-12 months.
  - **Run**: advanced controls that require mature integrations, advanced analytics, and comprehensive data coverage. Target: complete within 12-24 months.
• **Maturity scoring**: each UC contributes to a maturity score based on its wave weight and deployment status:
  - Crawl UCs: weight 3 (most impactful — foundational compliance)
  - Walk UCs: weight 2 (moderate impact — deeper coverage)
  - Run UCs: weight 1 (refinement and advanced detection)
  - Deployment status: deployed_and_running (1.0), deployed_not_running (0.5), not_deployed (0.0)
  - UC maturity = weight × deployment_status
  - Overall maturity = sum(UC maturity) / sum(max_possible_maturity) × 100
• **Data sources**:
  - `nis2_uc_catalogue.csv` — the complete UC catalogue with deployment status. Columns: `uc_id`, `title`, `wave` (crawl/walk/run), `article_ref`, `savedsearch_name` (the Splunk saved search — empty if not deployed), `deployment_status` (deployed_and_running, deployed_not_running, not_deployed), `deployment_date`, `data_source_status` (all_connected, partial, none), `owner`.
  - `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler` — verifies whether deployed searches are actually running.
  - `| rest /services/saved/searches` — enumerates all saved searches to cross-reference with the catalogue.
• **Next-best-control algorithm**: identifies the not-yet-deployed UC with the highest impact/effort ratio. Impact = wave weight × article coverage (how many Art.21 requirements the UC addresses). Effort = difficulty rating (from the UC's `difficulty` field: basic=1, intermediate=2, advanced=3). `next_best_score = impact / effort`. The UC with the highest score is the recommended next implementation.
• **RBAC**: role `nis2_programme_manager` with `srchIndexesAllowed = _internal, audit_evidence`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**UC catalogue CSV:**
Place `nis2_uc_catalogue.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-maturity/lookups/`. Populate with all 22.2.x UCs. The `deployment_status` field must be kept current by the NIS2 programme manager.

Validate: `| inputlookup nis2_uc_catalogue.csv | stats count dc(wave) as waves dc(article_ref) as articles values(wave) as wave_list values(deployment_status) as statuses`.

**Automated deployment verification:**
```spl
| inputlookup nis2_uc_catalogue.csv where deployment_status!="not_deployed"
| join type=left savedsearch_name [| rest /services/saved/searches splunk_server=local | rename title as savedsearch_name | table savedsearch_name disabled]
| eval actual_status=case(
    isnull(disabled), "NOT_IN_SPLUNK",
    disabled=1, "DISABLED",
    disabled=0, "ACTIVE")
| where actual_status != "ACTIVE" AND deployment_status="deployed_and_running"
| table uc_id title savedsearch_name deployment_status actual_status
```
This detects discrepancies between the catalogue (claims deployed) and reality (search is disabled or missing).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.57 NIS2 Maturity Benchmark`.

Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

• **Maturity score calculation**:
```spl
| inputlookup nis2_uc_catalogue.csv
| eval weight=case(wave="crawl",3, wave="walk",2, wave="run",1, 1==1, 1)
| eval status_factor=case(deployment_status="deployed_and_running",1.0, deployment_status="deployed_not_running",0.5, 1==1, 0.0)
| eval uc_score=weight * status_factor
| eval max_score=weight * 1.0
| stats sum(uc_score) as achieved sum(max_score) as possible count as total_ucs dc(eval(if(deployment_status="deployed_and_running",uc_id,null()))) as active_ucs by wave
| eval maturity_pct=round(100*achieved/possible, 1)
| append [| inputlookup nis2_uc_catalogue.csv | eval weight=case(wave="crawl",3,wave="walk",2,wave="run",1,1==1,1) | eval status_factor=case(deployment_status="deployed_and_running",1.0,deployment_status="deployed_not_running",0.5,1==1,0.0) | eval uc_score=weight*status_factor | eval max_score=weight*1.0 | stats sum(uc_score) as achieved sum(max_score) as possible count as total_ucs dc(eval(if(deployment_status="deployed_and_running",uc_id,null()))) as active_ucs | eval wave="OVERALL" | eval maturity_pct=round(100*achieved/possible,1)]
| table wave total_ucs active_ucs achieved possible maturity_pct
```

• **Next-best-control recommendation**:
```spl
| inputlookup nis2_uc_catalogue.csv where deployment_status="not_deployed"
| eval impact=case(wave="crawl",3, wave="walk",2, wave="run",1)
| eval effort=case(difficulty="basic",1, difficulty="intermediate",2, difficulty="advanced",3, 1==1, 2)
| eval nbc_score=round(impact/effort, 2)
| sort - nbc_score
| head 5
| table uc_id title wave article_ref difficulty nbc_score data_source_status
```
This identifies the top 5 UCs that would provide the most compliance improvement per unit of implementation effort.

Schedule: cron `0 8 1 * *` (monthly, first day at 08:00). Maturity changes slowly. Trigger: `maturity_pct < 50 for crawl wave` (all crawl UCs should be deployed first).

Alert actions: (1) For crawl maturity < 50%: email to the CISO and NIS2 programme manager — foundational compliance gaps. (2) Monthly summary email with overall maturity and top-5 next-best-controls. (3) Write scorecard to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:maturity:scorecard`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Maturity calculation**: manually count deployed UCs by wave. Compare with the SPL output. The `maturity_pct` should match the manual calculation.

(b) **Deployment verification**: intentionally set `deployment_status=deployed_and_running` for a UC whose saved search doesn't exist in Splunk. Run the automated verification search. Confirm the discrepancy is detected.

(c) **Next-best-control ranking**: verify the top recommendation makes sense. A crawl-wave, basic-difficulty UC with all data sources connected should rank highest.

(d) **Wave progression**: verify that crawl UCs are prioritised before walk, and walk before run. A run-wave UC should never be the top recommendation if crawl UCs are still not deployed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Maturity and Next-Best-Control`):
• **Row 1 — Maturity headline**: Single-value `Overall NIS2 maturity %` (green ≥ 80%, amber 50-79%, red < 50%). Single-value `Crawl maturity %`. Single-value `Walk maturity %`. Single-value `Run maturity %`.
• **Row 2 — Wave breakdown**: stacked bar chart or table showing `wave`, `total_ucs`, `active_ucs`, `maturity_pct`. Visual progress per wave.
• **Row 3 — Art.21 coverage matrix**: table showing each Art.21(2) clause (a) through (j), the number of UCs covering it, and the percentage that are deployed. Gaps reveal which NIS2 requirements have the weakest coverage.
• **Row 4 — Next-best-control recommendations**: table of top-5 UCs to implement next, with `nbc_score`, `wave`, `difficulty`, and `data_source_status`. If `data_source_status=none`, the data source must be connected before the UC can be deployed.

Runbook (owner: NIS2 Programme Manager / CISO):
1. Monthly: review the maturity dashboard. Present to the CISO with the top-3 next-best-controls.
2. Quarterly: present the maturity trend to the board. Show crawl/walk/run progression over time.
3. After deploying a new UC: update the `nis2_uc_catalogue.csv` with `deployment_status=deployed_and_running`, `deployment_date`, and `savedsearch_name`. Run the maturity search to verify the score increased.
4. Annually: recalibrate the catalogue. Review wave assignments based on evolving threat landscape and NIS2 implementing acts.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Catalogue out of sync with actual deployment.** The CSV says "not_deployed" but the search exists and is running. Fix: run the deployment verification search monthly and update the CSV to match reality.

• **Maturity percentage doesn't improve despite deployments.** The programme is deploying run-wave UCs (weight 1) while crawl-wave gaps (weight 3) remain. Fix: the next-best-control algorithm already prioritises crawl UCs. Follow its recommendations.

• **`difficulty` field not populated in the catalogue.** Without difficulty, the next-best-control score defaults to effort=2 (intermediate). Fix: review and populate the `difficulty` field for all UCs based on the SPL complexity and data source requirements.

• **Multiple articles per UC confuse the coverage matrix.** Some UCs satisfy multiple Art.21(2) clauses. Fix: if using a simple count, a UC covering both Art.21(2)(a) and Art.21(2)(d) should contribute to both counts. Use `mvexpand` on a multivalue `article_ref` field.
grandmaExplanation: We turn a long list of cyber duties into a simple priority list—who owns each item, how current the proof is, and what to improve next—so leaders spend their limited time on what actually moves the needle.
